declarations.doc: Conditional mutual information `I(X; Y | Z)` for finite-valued random variables.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfoOf

declarations.doc: Mutual information is symmetric under swapping the two coordinates of a joint law.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfo_map_swap

declarations.doc: Mutual information of finite-valued random variables is symmetric: `I(Y;X) = I(X;Y)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfoOf_swap

declarations.doc: Conditional mutual information is symmetric in its first two coordinates: `I(B;A | C) = I(A;B | C)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfo_map_swap12

declarations.doc: Conditional mutual information of finite-valued random variables is symmetric in the first two variables: `I(Y;X | Z) = I(X;Y | Z)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfoOf_swap

declarations.doc: Entropy of the first coordinate projection is entropy of the first marginal.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropyOf_fst

declarations.doc: Entropy of the second coordinate projection is entropy of the second marginal.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropyOf_snd

declarations.doc: The joint entropy of the two coordinate projections of an existing pair law is the entropy of that pair law itself.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.jointEntropyOf_fst_snd

declarations.doc: Conditional entropy of the coordinate projections of a pair law recovers the PMF-level conditional entropy of that pair law.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropyOf_fst_snd

declarations.doc: Mutual information of the coordinate projections of a pair law recovers the PMF-level mutual information of that pair law.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfoOf_fst_snd

declarations.doc: Entropy of the third coordinate projection is entropy of the third marginal.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropyOf_third

declarations.doc: Joint entropy of the first and third coordinate projections is the entropy of the first-third marginal.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.jointEntropyOf_fst_third

declarations.doc: Joint entropy of the second and third coordinate projections is the entropy of the second-third marginal.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.jointEntropyOf_snd_third

declarations.doc: Conditional mutual information of the coordinate projections of a triple law recovers the PMF-level `I(first; second | third)` quantity.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfoOf_fst_snd_third

declarations.doc: Unfolding lemma for the entropy-identity definition of conditional entropy.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropy_eq

declarations.doc: Unfolding lemma for the entropy-identity definition of mutual information.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfo_eq

declarations.doc: Unfolding lemma for the entropy-identity definition of conditional mutual information.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfo_eq

declarations.doc: Conditional entropy chain rule for a joint PMF: `H(A,B) = H(B) + H(A | B)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropy_eq_entropy_sndMarginal_add_condEntropy

declarations.doc: Swapped conditional entropy chain rule for a joint PMF: `H(A,B) = H(A) + H(B | A)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropy_eq_entropy_fstMarginal_add_condEntropy_swap

declarations.doc: Conditional entropy chain rule for finite-valued random variables: `H(X,Y) = H(Y) + H(X | Y)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.jointEntropyOf_eq_entropyOf_add_condEntropyOf

declarations.doc: Swapped conditional entropy chain rule for finite-valued random variables: `H(X,Y) = H(X) + H(Y | X)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.jointEntropyOf_eq_entropyOf_add_condEntropyOf_swap

declarations.doc: Right-oriented PMF entropy chain rule: `H(A,B) = H(B) + H(A|B)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropy_chain_rule_right

declarations.doc: Left-oriented PMF entropy chain rule: `H(A,B) = H(A) + H(B|A)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropy_chain_rule_left

declarations.doc: Right-oriented random-variable entropy chain rule.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.jointEntropyOf_chain_rule_right

declarations.doc: Left-oriented random-variable entropy chain rule.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.jointEntropyOf_chain_rule_left

declarations.doc: Random-variable conditional entropy rewrites to joint entropy minus entropy of the conditioning variable.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropyOf_eq

declarations.doc: Conditional joint entropy is invariant under swapping the two variables being conditioned: `H(Y,X|Z) = H(X,Y|Z)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropyOf_pair_swap

declarations.doc: Triple conditional-entropy chain rule: `H(X,Y|Z) = H(Y|Z) + H(X|Y,Z)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropyOf_pair_chain_rule

declarations.doc: Swapped triple conditional-entropy chain rule: `H(X,Y|Z) = H(X|Z) + H(Y|X,Z)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropyOf_pair_chain_rule_swap

declarations.doc: Random-variable mutual information rewrites to the standard entropy identity `H(X) + H(Y) - H(X,Y)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfoOf_eq

declarations.doc: Random-variable conditional mutual information rewrites to the entropy identity `H(X,Z) + H(Y,Z) - H(Z) - H(X,Y,Z)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfoOf_eq

declarations.doc: Conditional mutual information as a difference of conditional entropies: `I(X;Y|Z) = H(X|Z) - H(X|Y,Z)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfoOf_eq_condEntropyOf_sub_condEntropyOf

declarations.doc: Symmetric conditional-entropy difference form: `I(X;Y|Z) = H(Y|Z) - H(Y|X,Z)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfoOf_eq_condEntropyOf_sub_condEntropyOf_swap

declarations.doc: Conditional mutual information as conditional marginal entropies minus conditional joint entropy: `I(X;Y|Z) = H(X|Z) + H(Y|Z) - H(X,Y|Z)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfoOf_eq_condEntropyOf_add_condEntropyOf_sub_condEntropyOf_pair

declarations.doc: Mutual information as `I(A;B) = H(A) - H(A|B)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfo_eq_entropy_fstMarginal_sub_condEntropy

declarations.doc: Mutual information as `I(A;B) = H(B) - H(B|A)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfo_eq_entropy_sndMarginal_sub_condEntropy_swap

declarations.doc: Random-variable form `I(X;Y) = H(X) - H(X|Y)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfoOf_eq_entropyOf_sub_condEntropyOf

declarations.doc: Symmetric random-variable form `I(X;Y) = H(Y) - H(Y|X)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfoOf_eq_entropyOf_sub_condEntropyOf_swap

declarations.doc: A diagonal joint law has mutual information equal to its source entropy.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfo_map_diag

declarations.doc: The mutual information of a finite-valued variable with itself is its entropy.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfoOf_self

declarations.doc: Self-information of an atom under a finite PMF, in nats. It is `-log p(a)` when the real mass of `a` is nonzero, and `0` on zero-mass atoms. The zero branch is the usual entropy convention that zero-probability outcomes contribute no expected information.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.selfInfo

declarations.doc: A zero-mass atom has zero self-information by convention.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.selfInfo_of_toReal_eq_zero

declarations.doc: A nonzero-mass atom has self-information `-log p(a)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.selfInfo_of_toReal_ne_zero

declarations.doc: Multiplying self-information by the atom mass recovers the `Real.negMulLog` entropy summand.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.toReal_mul_selfInfo

declarations.doc: Semantic bridge for finite entropy: the finite-sum definition of `Shannon.entropy` is the expected self-information with respect to `PMF.toMeasure`. Informally, this is the textbook identity `H(P) = E[-log P(X)]`, with the zero-mass convention handled by `selfInfo`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropy_eq_integral_selfInfo

declarations.doc: Conditional law of the first coordinate given a nonzero second-coordinate atom. Mathematically, this is the finite PMF `P_{A | B=b}(a) = P_{A,B}(a,b) / P_B(b)`. The proof argument `hb : sndMarginal p b ≠ 0` is part of the API so that zero-probability conditioning atoms do not receive a fake default distribution.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condFstGivenSnd

declarations.doc: Atom formula for the conditional law: `P_{A | B=b}(a) = P_{A,B}(a,b) / P_B(b)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condFstGivenSnd_apply

declarations.doc: The conditional PMF does not depend on which proof of nonzero marginal mass was used to construct it.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condFstGivenSnd_proof_irrel

declarations.doc: An atom has zero conditional mass exactly when the corresponding joint atom has zero mass.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condFstGivenSnd_apply_eq_zero_iff

declarations.doc: An atom has nonzero conditional mass exactly when the corresponding joint atom has nonzero mass.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condFstGivenSnd_apply_ne_zero_iff

declarations.doc: The support of `P_{A | B=b}` is the fiber of the joint support over `b`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.support_condFstGivenSnd

declarations.doc: Joint mass factors as conditional mass times the conditioning marginal: `P_{A | B=b}(a) * P_B(b) = P_{A,B}(a,b)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condFstGivenSnd_mul_sndMarginal

declarations.doc: Joint mass factors as the conditioning marginal times conditional mass: `P_B(b) * P_{A | B=b}(a) = P_{A,B}(a,b)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.sndMarginal_mul_condFstGivenSnd

declarations.doc: Real-valued version of the joint factorization: `P_B(b) * P_{A | B=b}(a) = P_{A,B}(a,b)` after converting masses to `Real`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.sndMarginal_toReal_mul_condFstGivenSnd_toReal

declarations.doc: Entropy of the conditional fiber, with explicit zero-mass branch. This helper is intended for expected conditional entropy formulas. If `P_B(b)=0`, the branch is the number `0`, not the entropy of an arbitrary default conditional PMF.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropyFstGivenSnd

declarations.doc: Zero-marginal conditioning atoms contribute zero to conditional-fiber entropy.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropyFstGivenSnd_of_sndMarginal_eq_zero

declarations.doc: On nonzero conditioning atoms, `condEntropyFstGivenSnd` is the entropy of the canonical conditional PMF `P_{A | B=b}`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropyFstGivenSnd_of_sndMarginal_ne_zero

declarations.doc: Per-fiber conditional entropy identity. For each conditioning atom `b`, `p_B(b) * H(P_{A | B=b}) = sum_a h(p(a,b)) - h(p_B(b))`, where `h(x) = -x log x`. If `p_B(b)=0`, the left side uses the explicit zero branch of `condEntropyFstGivenSnd`, and the right side is zero because every joint atom over `b` has zero mass.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.sndMarginal_toReal_mul_condEntropyFstGivenSnd

declarations.doc: Expected conditional entropy formula for the finite conditional-law API: `H(A | B) = sum_b p_B(b) * H(P_{A | B=b})`. The right-hand side is written using `condEntropyFstGivenSnd`, whose value is `0` on zero-marginal conditioning atoms and the entropy of the canonical conditional PMF on nonzero atoms.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropy_eq_sum_sndMarginal_mul_condEntropyFstGivenSnd

declarations.doc: The right-associated law `P_{A,B,C}` viewed as a law on `((A, B), C)`. This is the law whose conditional fibers over `C=c` are joint laws on `A × B`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.pairThirdLaw

declarations.doc: Atom formula for the reassociated law `((A,B),C)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.pairThirdLaw_apply

declarations.doc: The second marginal of the reassociated law `((A,B),C)` is the law of `C`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.sndMarginal_pairThirdLaw

declarations.doc: Reassociating `(A,B,C)` as `((A,B),C)` preserves entropy.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropy_pairThirdLaw

declarations.doc: PMF form of the triple conditional-entropy chain rule: `H(A,B|C) = H(B|C) + H(A|B,C)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropy_pairThirdLaw_chain_rule

declarations.doc: Swapped PMF form of the triple conditional-entropy chain rule: `H(A,B|C) = H(A|C) + H(B|A,C)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropy_pairThirdLaw_chain_rule_swap

declarations.doc: Conditional joint law of the first two coordinates given a nonzero third coordinate atom. Mathematically, this is `P_{A,B | C=c}(a,b) = P_{A,B,C}(a,b,c) / P_C(c)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condFstSndGivenThird

declarations.doc: Atom formula for `P_{A,B | C=c}`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condFstSndGivenThird_apply

declarations.doc: Joint mass factors through the conditional joint law: `P_C(c) * P_{A,B|C=c}(a,b) = P_{A,B,C}(a,b,c)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.thirdMarginal_mul_condFstSndGivenThird

declarations.doc: Real-valued joint factorization for the conditional joint law: `P_C(c) * P_{A,B|C=c}(a,b) = P_{A,B,C}(a,b,c)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.thirdMarginal_toReal_mul_condFstSndGivenThird_toReal

declarations.doc: The second marginal of the `(A,C)` law is the law of `C`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.sndMarginal_fstThirdMarginal

declarations.doc: The second marginal of the `(B,C)` law is the law of `C`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.sndMarginal_sndThirdMarginal

declarations.doc: The first marginal of `P_{A,B|C=c}` is the conditional law `P_{A|C=c}`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.fstMarginal_condFstSndGivenThird

declarations.doc: The second marginal of `P_{A,B|C=c}` is the conditional law `P_{B|C=c}`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.sndMarginal_condFstSndGivenThird

declarations.doc: The conditional law of `(A,B)` given `C=c`, obtained by conditioning the reassociated law `((A,B),C)`, is the same as `condFstSndGivenThird`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condFstGivenSnd_pairThirdLaw_eq_condFstSndGivenThird

declarations.doc: Conditional mutual information of the fiber over `C=c`. The value is zero when `P_C(c)=0`; otherwise it is the mutual information of the conditional joint law `P_{A,B|C=c}`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfoFstSndGivenThird

declarations.doc: Zero-marginal conditioning atoms contribute zero fiber mutual information.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfoFstSndGivenThird_of_thirdMarginal_eq_zero

declarations.doc: On nonzero third-coordinate atoms, the fiber CMI is the mutual information of `P_{A,B|C=c}`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfoFstSndGivenThird_of_thirdMarginal_ne_zero

declarations.doc: Conditional mutual information can be rewritten as `H(A|C) + H(B|C) - H(A,B|C)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfo_eq_condEntropy_marginals

declarations.doc: On a nonzero third-coordinate atom, fiber CMI is the entropy identity for the three conditional fibers: `I(A;B | C=c) = H(A|C=c) + H(B|C=c) - H(A,B|C=c)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfoFstSndGivenThird_eq_entropy_fibers_of_ne_zero

declarations.doc: Weighted pointwise bridge between fiber CMI and the three conditional entropy fibers. The zero-marginal case is killed by the factor `P_C(c)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.thirdMarginal_toReal_mul_condMutualInfoFstSndGivenThird

declarations.doc: Expected conditional mutual information formula: `I(A;B | C) = sum_c P_C(c) I(A;B | C=c)`. The fiber term is zero on zero-marginal `c` and otherwise the mutual information of `P_{A,B|C=c}`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfo_eq_sum_thirdMarginal_mul_condMutualInfoFstSndGivenThird

declarations.doc: The mathlib kernel associated to a countable PMF-valued channel. This is a semantic view of the existing raw channel function, not a second public channel representation.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.pmfChannelKernel

declarations.doc: Evaluating the kernel associated to a PMF channel recovers its PMF measure.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.pmfChannelKernel_apply

declarations.doc: The measure of an induced PMF joint law is the composition-product of the input measure and the associated Markov kernel.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.channelJoint_toMeasure

declarations.doc: The total posterior channel associated to an input law and a finite stochastic channel. On an output atom of positive probability this is the usual posterior input law. On a null output fiber it uses the fallback already documented by `condFstGivenSndChannel`; reconstruction makes that arbitrary branch semantically irrelevant.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.channelPosterior

declarations.doc: Sampling the posterior from the channel output law reconstructs the induced joint law in output-input order.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.channelPosterior_reconstructs_joint

declarations.doc: Exact KL decomposition for two input laws passed through the same finite stochastic channel. The first term is the divergence between the output laws. The remainder is the divergence between the two posterior kernels over the first output law, stated with mathlib composition-product measures. No support-inclusion assumption is needed because the identity is `ENNReal`-valued.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.klDiv_channel_eq_add_posterior

declarations.doc: Finite relative entropy contracts when two input laws pass through the same stochastic channel. This is the primary channel data-processing inequality. It is unconditional because KL divergence is valued in `ENNReal`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.klDiv_channel_le

declarations.doc: Real-valued finite relative entropy contracts through a common stochastic channel when the first input law is supported by the second. Only the input support condition is needed; no separate support hypothesis on the output laws is required.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.toReal_klDiv_channel_le

declarations.doc: KL data processing for a deterministic map of a finite alphabet.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.klDiv_map_le

declarations.doc: Real-valued KL data processing for a deterministic map, under input support inclusion.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.toReal_klDiv_map_le

declarations.doc: Appending a common channel stage cannot increase the KL divergence between the two intermediate output laws.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.klDiv_channelComp_le

declarations.doc: Textbook-facing alias for `ENNReal`-valued KL contraction along a cascade.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.klDiv_channel_cascade_le

declarations.doc: Real-valued KL contraction for a channel cascade. The original input support condition suffices for the intermediate laws as well.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.toReal_klDiv_channelComp_le

declarations.doc: Textbook-facing alias for real-valued KL contraction along a cascade.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.toReal_klDiv_channel_cascade_le

declarations.doc: KL divergence to an invariant reference law cannot increase after one step of a finite stochastic channel.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.klDiv_channel_invariant_le

declarations.doc: Real-valued KL divergence to an invariant reference law contracts when the input law is supported by that reference.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.toReal_klDiv_channel_invariant_le

declarations.doc: A finite channel that preserves the uniform law cannot decrease Shannon entropy. The discrete measurable structure used by the KL proof remains local, so the statement has only the natural finite nonempty alphabet assumptions.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropy_le_entropy_bind_of_uniform_invariant

declarations.doc: A finite doubly stochastic channel cannot decrease Shannon entropy. Each `W a` is already a probability law, hence supplies the row-stochastic condition. The displayed hypothesis is the remaining column-sum condition. No transition-matrix representation is introduced.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropy_le_entropy_bind_of_doublyStochastic

declarations.doc: Real-valued first-marginal mass formula. Mathematically, for a finite joint PMF `p` on `alpha × beta`, `p_A(a) = sum_b p(a,b)`, now stated after converting all finite masses to real numbers.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.fstMarginal_toReal_apply

declarations.doc: Real-valued second-marginal mass formula. Mathematically, for a finite joint PMF `p` on `alpha × beta`, `p_B(b) = sum_a p(a,b)`, now stated after converting all finite masses to real numbers.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.sndMarginal_toReal_apply

declarations.doc: Rewrite an expectation of a first-coordinate observable under the joint law as an expectation under the first marginal: `sum_{a,b} p(a,b) f(a) = sum_a p_A(a) f(a)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.sum_toReal_mul_fstMarginal

declarations.doc: Rewrite an expectation of a second-coordinate observable under the joint law as an expectation under the second marginal: `sum_{a,b} p(a,b) f(b) = sum_b p_B(b) f(b)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.sum_toReal_mul_sndMarginal

declarations.doc: Entropy as the elementary finite sum `sum_a p(a) * (-log p(a))`. This is definitionally the same as the `Real.negMulLog` formula, but this shape is better for comparing entropy identities with KL-divergence sums.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropy_eq_sum_toReal_mul_neg_log

declarations.doc: First-marginal entropy as a joint-law expectation: `H(A) = sum_{a,b} p(a,b) * (-log p_A(a))`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropy_fstMarginal_eq_sum_joint

declarations.doc: Second-marginal entropy as a joint-law expectation: `H(B) = sum_{a,b} p(a,b) * (-log p_B(b))`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropy_sndMarginal_eq_sum_joint

declarations.doc: Mutual information written as the joint expectation of the three entropy summands: `I(A;B) = sum_{a,b} p(a,b) * (-log p_A(a) + -log p_B(b) - -log p(a,b))`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfo_eq_sum_neg_log

declarations.doc: Pointwise algebra behind the KL formula for mutual information. For every atom `(a,b)`, the weighted entropy summand equals `p(a,b) * log (p(a,b) / (p_A(a) * p_B(b)))`. If `p(a,b)=0`, both sides are zero; otherwise nonzero joint mass forces both marginal masses to be nonzero.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfo_summand_eq_log_ratio

declarations.doc: Finite log-ratio formula for mutual information: `I(A;B) = sum_{a,b} p(a,b) log (p(a,b) / (p_A(a) p_B(b)))`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfo_eq_sum_log_ratio

declarations.doc: A joint PMF is independent when it is the product of its marginals.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.IsIndependent

declarations.doc: Two random variables are independent when their joint law is independent.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.IsIndependentOf

declarations.doc: The first two coordinates of a triple PMF are conditionally independent given the third when every atom satisfies the cross-product factorization `p(a,b,c) * p_C(c) = p_AC(a,c) * p_BC(b,c)`. This definition is meaningful on null fibers and does not require choosing a conditional PMF from a proof that `p_C(c) ≠ 0`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.IsCondIndependent

declarations.doc: Two random variables are conditionally independent given a third when their mapped triple law is conditionally independent.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.IsCondIndependentOf

declarations.doc: Swapping the first two coordinates of a triple law preserves conditional independence.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.isCondIndependent_map_swap12

declarations.doc: Conditional independence of random variables is symmetric in its first two variables.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.isCondIndependentOf_swap

declarations.doc: Independence is equivalent to pointwise factorization into marginal masses.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.isIndependent_iff_apply_eq_mul_marginals

declarations.doc: The independent product of two PMFs is independent.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.isIndependent_indepProd

declarations.doc: Swapping the coordinates of a joint law preserves independence.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.isIndependent_map_swap

declarations.doc: Random-variable independence is equality of the joint law with the independent product of the two individual laws.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.isIndependentOf_iff_map_eq_indepProd

declarations.doc: The PMF mapped-law definition of random-variable independence agrees with mathlib's measure-theoretic `ProbabilityTheory.IndepFun` predicate. Measurability and measurable-singleton assumptions are explicit here because they belong to the semantic bridge, not to the PMF-first definition `IsIndependentOf`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.isIndependentOf_iff_indepFun

declarations.doc: Independence of random variables is symmetric.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.isIndependentOf_swap

declarations.doc: Finite mutual information is zero exactly when the joint PMF is independent. The proof chooses the discrete measurable structure only locally, uses the KL representation of mutual information, and discharges the necessary real-KL finiteness guard through support inclusion.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfo_eq_zero_iff_isIndependent

declarations.doc: Finite-valued random variables have zero mutual information exactly when their mapped joint law is independent.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfoOf_eq_zero_iff_isIndependentOf

declarations.doc: Conditioning preserves the entropy of the left coordinate exactly when the two coordinates are independent.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropy_eq_entropy_left_iff_isIndependent

declarations.doc: Conditioning preserves `H(X)` exactly when the finite-valued random variables `X` and `Y` are independent.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropyOf_eq_entropyOf_iff_isIndependentOf

declarations.doc: Joint entropy is additive exactly when the two coordinates are independent.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.jointEntropy_eq_add_marginalEntropy_iff_isIndependent

declarations.doc: Joint entropy is additive exactly when the finite-valued random variables are independent.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.jointEntropyOf_eq_add_entropyOf_iff_isIndependentOf

declarations.doc: Short PMF alias for additivity of joint entropy under independence.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.jointEntropy_additive_iff_isIndependent

declarations.doc: Short random-variable alias for additivity of joint entropy under independence.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.jointEntropyOf_additive_iff_isIndependentOf

declarations.doc: Finite conditional mutual information is zero exactly when every positive-mass conditional fiber has zero mutual information. The forward direction uses nonnegativity to extract zero from each term in the averaged conditional-mutual-information formula. The marginal guard lets us cancel the positive real fiber weight. In the reverse direction, null fibers have zero weight and every positive-mass fiber is zero by hypothesis.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfo_eq_zero_iff_condMutualInfoFstSndGivenThird_eq_zero

declarations.doc: The cross-product definition of conditional independence is equivalent to ordinary independence of the conditional joint law on every positive-mass fiber. No condition is needed on null fibers: the cross-product identity holds there because the corresponding first-third marginal has zero mass. The right-hand side is independent of the particular proof of nonzero marginal mass.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.isCondIndependent_iff_isIndependent_condFstSndGivenThird

declarations.doc: Finite conditional mutual information is zero exactly when the first two coordinates are conditionally independent given the third. No conditional law needs to be chosen on null fibers. `IsCondIndependent` uses the cross-product identity on the original PMF, where those fibers satisfy the identity directly; conditional PMFs appear only for positive-mass fibers inside the proof.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfo_eq_zero_iff_isCondIndependent

declarations.doc: Finite-valued random variables have zero conditional mutual information exactly when they are conditionally independent under their mapped triple law.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfoOf_eq_zero_iff_isCondIndependentOf

declarations.doc: Conditioning the first coordinate on the second preserves its entropy given the third coordinate exactly under conditional independence.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropy_eq_condEntropy_fstThirdMarginal_iff_isCondIndependent

declarations.doc: Conditioning `X` on `Y` preserves its entropy given `Z` exactly when `X` and `Y` are conditionally independent given `Z`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropyOf_eq_condEntropyOf_iff_isCondIndependentOf

declarations.doc: Conditional joint entropy is additive exactly when the first two coordinates are conditionally independent given the third.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropy_pair_eq_add_condEntropy_iff_isCondIndependent

declarations.doc: Conditional joint entropy of finite-valued random variables is additive exactly under conditional independence.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropyOf_pair_eq_add_condEntropyOf_iff_isCondIndependentOf

declarations.doc: Short PMF alias for conditional joint-entropy additivity.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropy_pair_additive_iff_isCondIndependent

declarations.doc: Short random-variable alias for conditional joint-entropy additivity.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropyOf_pair_additive_iff_isCondIndependentOf

declarations.doc: Absolute continuity between PMF measures is exactly inclusion of their set-valued supports. This statement does not require a finite alphabet: measurable singletons let the forward direction test individual atoms, while the reverse direction uses the PMF measure's zero-set characterization.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.toMeasure_absolutelyContinuous_iff_support_subset

declarations.doc: On a finite alphabet, PMF KL divergence is finite exactly when the first PMF's support is contained in the second PMF's support.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.klDiv_pmf_ne_top_iff_support_subset

declarations.doc: On a finite alphabet, PMF KL divergence is infinite exactly when support inclusion fails.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.klDiv_pmf_eq_top_iff_not_support_subset

declarations.doc: KL divergence between two PMFs is zero exactly when the PMFs are equal. The alphabet need not be finite: PMF measures are finite measures, and measurable singletons make `PMF.toMeasure` injective.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.klDiv_pmf_eq_zero_iff

declarations.doc: On a finite alphabet and under support inclusion, real-valued PMF KL divergence is zero exactly when the PMFs are equal. The support hypothesis is essential: without KL finiteness, `ENNReal.toReal ⊤ = 0` would make the reverse characterization false.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.toReal_klDiv_pmf_eq_zero_iff

declarations.doc: Countable PMF density formula. If `p.toMeasure ≪ q.toMeasure`, then `p.toMeasure` is obtained from `q.toMeasure` by the density `a ↦ p(a) / q(a)`. On atoms with `q(a)=0`, absolute continuity forces `p(a)=0`, so the formula is still valid.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.toMeasure_withDensity_pmf_div

declarations.doc: Finite KL expansion for absolutely continuous PMFs: `D(P || Q) = sum_a p(a) log (p(a) / q(a))`. The left-hand side is mathlib's measure-theoretic KL divergence applied to `PMF.toMeasure`; the right-hand side is the usual finite sum over atoms.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.toReal_klDiv_pmf_eq_sum

declarations.doc: KL divergence from a finite PMF to the uniform law on a nonempty finset is the uniform log-cardinality minus entropy, provided the PMF is supported on that finset: `D(P || U_s) = log |s| - H(P)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.toReal_klDiv_pmf_uniformOfFinset

declarations.doc: Full-alphabet form of `toReal_klDiv_pmf_uniformOfFinset`: `D(P || U_alpha) = log |alpha| - H(P)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.toReal_klDiv_pmf_uniformOfFintype

declarations.doc: Semantic bridge for finite mutual information: `I(A;B) = D(P_AB || P_A × P_B)`. Here `P_A × P_B` is represented by the finite independent-product PMF `indepProd (fstMarginal p) (sndMarginal p)`. This theorem is the finite textbook identity connecting the entropy definition of mutual information to mathlib's measure-theoretic KL divergence.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfo_eq_toReal_klDiv_joint_indepProd

declarations.doc: Product-measure form of the finite mutual-information KL bridge: `I(A;B) = D(P_AB || P_A.toMeasure.prod P_B.toMeasure)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfo_eq_toReal_klDiv_joint_prod_marginals

declarations.doc: Fiberwise KL form of conditional mutual information. This is zero on zero-marginal third-coordinate atoms. On nonzero atoms it is `D(P_{A,B|C=c} || P_{A|C=c} × P_{B|C=c})`, using the marginals of the conditional joint law.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfoFstSndGivenThirdKL

declarations.doc: The fiber mutual-information helper agrees with the fiberwise KL expression `D(P_{A,B|C=c} || P_{A|C=c} × P_{B|C=c})`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfoFstSndGivenThird_eq_KL

declarations.doc: Averaged conditional-KL bridge for finite conditional mutual information: `I(A;B | C) = sum_c P_C(c) D(P_{A,B|C=c} || P_{A|C=c} × P_{B|C=c})`. Zero-marginal `c` contribute zero by definition of `condMutualInfoFstSndGivenThirdKL`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfo_eq_sum_thirdMarginal_mul_condMutualInfoFstSndGivenThirdKL

declarations.doc: Random variables `X`, `Y`, and `Z` form the Markov chain `X → Y → Z` when `X` and `Z` are conditionally independent given `Y`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.IsMarkovChainOf

declarations.doc: A triple PMF is a Markov chain in its first-to-second-to-third coordinate order.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.IsMarkovChain

declarations.doc: A triple law is Markov in coordinate order exactly when every atom satisfies the cross-product factorization `p(a,b,c) * p_B(b) = p_AB(a,b) * p_BC(b,c)`. The identity also covers null middle-coordinate fibers, so it requires no finite-alphabet or measurable-space assumptions.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.isMarkovChain_iff_crossProduct

declarations.doc: A triple law is Markov exactly when its two endpoint coordinates are independent in every positive-mass conditional fiber of the middle coordinate. The displayed mapped law orders the coordinates as endpoint, endpoint, middle so that it can reuse `condFstSndGivenThird` directly.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.isMarkovChain_iff_isIndependent_condFstSndGivenThird

declarations.doc: Textbook-facing alias for the positive-fiber Markov characterization.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.isMarkovChain_iff_fiberwise_endpoints_independent

declarations.doc: The Markov condition `X → Y → Z` is equivalent to the textbook zero-CMI identity `I(X;Z | Y) = 0`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfoOf_eq_zero_iff_isMarkovChainOf

declarations.doc: Reversing a Markov chain preserves the random-variable Markov condition.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.isMarkovChainOf_reverse

declarations.doc: Reversing the coordinates of a triple law preserves its Markov condition.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.isMarkovChain_map_reverse

declarations.doc: The total channel from the second coordinate to the first associated with a joint PMF. For `P_B(b) != 0`, this is the canonical conditional law `P_{A | B=b}`. For a null fiber, it is the first marginal `P_A`. The fallback supplies a PMF without requiring an additional `Nonempty alpha` assumption; it has no intended conditional semantics and disappears from the weighted reconstruction laws.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condFstGivenSndChannel

declarations.doc: On a null conditioning fiber, the total channel uses the documented fallback.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condFstGivenSndChannel_of_sndMarginal_eq_zero

declarations.doc: On a positive conditioning fiber, the total channel is the canonical conditional PMF.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condFstGivenSndChannel_of_sndMarginal_ne_zero

declarations.doc: The value chosen for a null conditional fiber is irrelevant after weighting by the conditioning marginal. The PMF `q` represents any alternative fallback.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condFstGivenSndChannel_null_fiber_irrelevant

declarations.doc: The conditioning marginal times the total conditional-channel mass reconstructs the original joint mass on every fiber, including null fibers.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.sndMarginal_mul_condFstGivenSndChannel

declarations.doc: Sampling the total conditional channel from the second marginal reconstructs the original pair law with its coordinates in channel input-output order.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.channelJoint_condFstGivenSndChannel

declarations.doc: Extending any pair law of `(A,B)` by sampling `C` from a channel depending only on `B` produces the Markov chain `A → B → C`. The result is type-generic and includes null middle-coordinate fibers through the cross-product characterization, without requiring a conditional PMF.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.isMarkovChain_channelExtension

declarations.doc: A finite triple law is Markov exactly when its first-two marginal, extended by the total conditional channel `P_{C | B}` extracted from its second-third marginal, reconstructs the original law. The second-third marginal is swapped into `(C,B)` order because `condFstGivenSndChannel` constructs a channel from the second coordinate to the first. Its documented null-fiber fallback is irrelevant: both the original and reconstructed atom masses vanish whenever the middle marginal does.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.isMarkovChain_iff_eq_channelExtension_condFstGivenSndChannel

declarations.doc: Compatibility alias for reconstruction by the canonical total conditional channel.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.isMarkovChain_iff_canonical_channelExtension

declarations.doc: Textbook channel-factorization characterization of a finite Markov triple: a law is Markov exactly when some channel from the middle alphabet extends its first-two marginal to the original triple law.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.isMarkovChain_iff_exists_channelExtension

declarations.doc: Exact mutual-information loss along a finite Markov triple: `I(A;B) = I(A;C) + I(A;B | C)`. This is the PMF form of the Markov chain rule. The Markov hypothesis removes the opposite conditional term `I(A;C | B)` from the ordinary chain rule.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfo_markov_chain_rule

declarations.doc: Exact mutual-information loss along the finite Markov chain `X → Y → Z`: `I(X;Y) = I(X;Z) + I(X;Y | Z)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfoOf_markov_chain_rule

declarations.doc: Mutual-information data processing for a finite Markov triple: `I(A;C) <= I(A;B)` when `A -> B -> C`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfo_dataProcessing

declarations.doc: Random-variable mutual-information data processing: `I(X;Z) <= I(X;Y)` when `X -> Y -> Z`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfoOf_dataProcessing

declarations.doc: Conditional-entropy form of data processing: `H(X|Y) <= H(X|Z)` when `X -> Y -> Z`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropyOf_dataProcessing

declarations.doc: PMF form of the conditional-entropy data-processing consequence: `H(A|B) <= H(A|C)` when `A -> B -> C`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropy_dataProcessing

declarations.doc: Equality in mutual-information data processing holds exactly when the reverse chain `X -> Z -> Y` is also Markov.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfoOf_dataProcessing_eq_iff

declarations.doc: For a Markov triple law, equality in mutual-information data processing holds exactly when its first, third, and second coordinate variables form the reverse Markov chain.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfo_dataProcessing_eq_iff

declarations.doc: Applying a channel to the right coordinate of a finite joint law cannot increase mutual information.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfo_channel_right_le

declarations.doc: Applying a channel to the left coordinate of a finite joint law cannot increase mutual information. The displayed bind is the law of the processed left output paired with the unchanged right coordinate.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfo_channel_left_le

declarations.doc: Applying independent channels to both coordinates of a finite joint law cannot increase mutual information. Conditional on an input pair `(a,b)`, the two channel outputs have law `indepProd (W a) (V b)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfo_independent_channels_le

declarations.doc: Mutual information contracts along a two-stage finite channel cascade.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfo_channelComp_le

declarations.doc: Textbook-facing alias for mutual-information contraction along a channel cascade.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfo_channel_cascade_le

declarations.doc: Deterministically mapping every output law of a finite channel cannot increase its input-output mutual information.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfo_channel_map_output_le

declarations.doc: Independent product of two probability mass functions. Mathematically, this is the law of `(X, Y)` when `X` has law `p`, `Y` has law `q`, and the two variables are independent.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.indepProd

declarations.doc: The atom mass of an independent product is the product of the two atom masses.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.indepProd_apply

declarations.doc: An atom of the independent product has zero mass exactly when one of the two coordinate atoms has zero mass.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.indepProd_apply_eq_zero_iff

declarations.doc: An atom belongs to the independent product support exactly when both coordinate atoms belong to the corresponding supports.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.indepProd_apply_ne_zero_iff

declarations.doc: The support of an independent product is the product of the two supports.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.support_indepProd

declarations.doc: The first marginal of an independent product is the first factor law.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.fstMarginal_indepProd

declarations.doc: The second marginal of an independent product is the second factor law.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.sndMarginal_indepProd

declarations.doc: Swapping the coordinates of an independent product swaps the two factor laws.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.indepProd_map_swap

declarations.doc: The independent product law assigns product rectangles the product of the factor probabilities.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.indepProd_toMeasure_prod

declarations.doc: The measure associated to the independent product PMF is the product of the two associated measures.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.indepProd_toMeasure

declarations.doc: A finite joint PMF is absolutely continuous with respect to the independent product of its two marginals. Mathematically, if the product of the marginal masses of `(a, b)` is zero, then one marginal atom is zero, hence the joint atom `p (a, b)` is zero. This is the support fact needed before comparing finite mutual information with KL divergence from the joint law to the product of its marginals.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.joint_toMeasure_absolutelyContinuous_indepProd_marginals

declarations.doc: Product-measure version of `joint_toMeasure_absolutelyContinuous_indepProd_marginals`. This is the form expected by KL-divergence statements that use `Measure.prod` directly.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.joint_toMeasure_absolutelyContinuous_prod_marginals

declarations.doc: Mutual information is nonnegative: `0 <= I(A;B)`. The proof passes through the semantic bridge `I(A;B) = D(P_AB || P_A x P_B)` and then uses nonnegativity of the real coercion of an `ENNReal` KL divergence.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfo_nonneg

declarations.doc: Fiber conditional entropy is nonnegative: `0 <= H(A | B=b)`. On zero-mass fibers this is `0 <= 0`; on nonzero fibers it reduces to entropy nonnegativity for the conditional law `P_{A|B=b}`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropyFstGivenSnd_nonneg

declarations.doc: On a nonzero conditioning fiber, conditional entropy is zero exactly when the canonical conditional PMF is pure.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropyFstGivenSnd_eq_zero_iff_of_sndMarginal_ne_zero

declarations.doc: Conditional entropy is nonnegative: `0 <= H(A | B)`. The proof uses the finite semantic bridge `H(A | B) = sum_b P_B(b) * H(P_{A|B=b})`, then every summand is nonnegative.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropy_nonneg

declarations.doc: Conditional entropy of finite-valued random variables is nonnegative.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropyOf_nonneg

declarations.doc: Conditional entropy is zero exactly when the first coordinate is a function of the second coordinate on the support of the joint PMF.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropy_eq_zero_iff_exists_function

declarations.doc: Conditional entropy of finite-valued random variables is zero exactly when the first variable is a function of the second on the source PMF support.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropyOf_eq_zero_iff_exists_function

declarations.doc: A deterministic function of `Y` has zero conditional entropy given `Y`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropyOf_comp_eq_zero

declarations.doc: A finite-valued random variable has zero conditional entropy given itself.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropyOf_self_eq_zero

declarations.doc: Joint entropy equals the entropy of the second marginal exactly when the first coordinate is a function of the second on the joint support.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropy_eq_entropy_sndMarginal_iff_exists_function

declarations.doc: `H(X,Y) = H(Y)` exactly when `X` is a function of `Y` on the source PMF support.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.jointEntropyOf_eq_entropyOf_iff_exists_function

declarations.doc: Applying a deterministic map to a finite PMF cannot increase entropy.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropy_map_le

declarations.doc: Applying a deterministic map preserves entropy exactly when it is injective on the support of the source PMF.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropy_map_eq_iff_injOn_support

declarations.doc: Deterministic post-processing of a finite-valued variable cannot increase entropy.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropyOf_comp_le

declarations.doc: Deterministic post-processing preserves random-variable entropy exactly when the map is injective on the support of the law of the original variable.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropyOf_comp_eq_iff_injOn_support

declarations.doc: Conditional deterministic chain rule: `H(X|Z) = H(f(X)|Z) + H(X|f(X),Z)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropyOf_deterministic_chain_rule

declarations.doc: Deterministic post-processing cannot increase conditional entropy.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropyOf_comp_le

declarations.doc: Deterministic post-processing preserves conditional entropy exactly when `X` is recoverable from `(f(X), Z)` on the source PMF support.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropyOf_comp_eq_iff_exists_recovery

declarations.doc: Applying a deterministic map to the first coordinate cannot increase `H(A|C)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropy_map_fst_le

declarations.doc: The first-coordinate map preserves `H(A|C)` exactly when `A` is recoverable from `(f(A), C)` on the joint support.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropy_map_fst_eq_iff_exists_recovery

declarations.doc: Conditioning reduces entropy: `H(A|B) <= H(A)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropy_le_entropy_fstMarginal

declarations.doc: Mutual information is at most the entropy of the first marginal.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfo_le_entropy_fstMarginal

declarations.doc: Mutual information is at most the entropy of the second marginal.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfo_le_entropy_sndMarginal

declarations.doc: The entropy of the first marginal is at most the joint entropy.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropy_fstMarginal_le_entropy

declarations.doc: The entropy of the second marginal is at most the joint entropy.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropy_sndMarginal_le_entropy

declarations.doc: Joint entropy is subadditive: `H(A,B) <= H(A) + H(B)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropy_le_entropy_fstMarginal_add_entropy_sndMarginal

declarations.doc: Random-variable conditioning reduces entropy: `H(X|Y) <= H(X)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropyOf_le_entropyOf

declarations.doc: Random-variable mutual information is at most `H(X)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfoOf_le_entropyOf_left

declarations.doc: Random-variable mutual information is at most `H(Y)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfoOf_le_entropyOf_right

declarations.doc: The entropy of `X` is at most the joint entropy `H(X,Y)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropyOf_le_jointEntropyOf

declarations.doc: The entropy of `Y` is at most the joint entropy `H(X,Y)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropyOf_le_jointEntropyOf_swap

declarations.doc: Random-variable joint entropy is subadditive: `H(X,Y) <= H(X) + H(Y)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.jointEntropyOf_le_entropyOf_add_entropyOf

declarations.doc: Textbook-facing PMF alias for `H(A|B) <= H(A)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropy_le_entropy_left

declarations.doc: Textbook-facing PMF alias for `I(A;B) <= H(A)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfo_le_entropy_left

declarations.doc: Textbook-facing PMF alias for `I(A;B) <= H(B)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfo_le_entropy_right

declarations.doc: Textbook-facing PMF alias for `H(A) <= H(A,B)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropy_left_le_jointEntropy

declarations.doc: Textbook-facing PMF alias for `H(B) <= H(A,B)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropy_right_le_jointEntropy

declarations.doc: Textbook-facing PMF alias for joint-entropy subadditivity.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.jointEntropy_le_add_marginalEntropy

declarations.doc: Left-oriented alias for `H(X) <= H(X,Y)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropyOf_left_le_jointEntropyOf

declarations.doc: Right-oriented alias for `H(Y) <= H(X,Y)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropyOf_right_le_jointEntropyOf

declarations.doc: Short random-variable alias for joint-entropy subadditivity.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.jointEntropyOf_le_add_entropyOf

declarations.doc: Fiber conditional mutual information is nonnegative: `0 <= I(A;B | C=c)`. On zero-mass fibers this is `0 <= 0`; on nonzero fibers it reduces to `mutualInfo_nonneg` applied to the conditional joint law `P_{A,B|C=c}`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfoFstSndGivenThird_nonneg

declarations.doc: Conditional mutual information is nonnegative: `0 <= I(A;B | C)`. We use the finite semantic bridge `I(A;B | C) = sum_c P_C(c) * I(A;B | C=c)`, then each summand is nonnegative because both `P_C(c)` and the fiber mutual information are nonnegative.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfo_nonneg

declarations.doc: Conditional mutual information of finite-valued random variables is nonnegative.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfoOf_nonneg

declarations.doc: Taking the first marginal after projecting `(A,B,C)` to `(A,C)` recovers the first marginal of the original law: `(P_AC)_A = P_A`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.fstMarginal_fstThirdMarginal

declarations.doc: Viewing a triple law as a joint law of `A` and `(B,C)`, the second marginal is the same law as the explicit `(B,C)` marginal: `P_{B,C} = sndMarginal P_{A,(B,C)}`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.sndMarginal_eq_sndThirdMarginal

declarations.doc: First mutual-information chain rule: `I(A;B,C) = I(A;C) + I(A;B | C)`. In Lean's right-associated product convention, a PMF `p : PMF (alpha × beta × gamma)` is also a joint law of `A` and `(B,C)`, so `mutualInfo p` denotes `I(A;B,C)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfo_chain_rule_fst

declarations.doc: Second mutual-information chain-rule variant: `I(A;B,C) = I(A;B) + I(A;C | B)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfo_chain_rule_snd

declarations.doc: Random-variable form of `mutualInfo_chain_rule_fst`: `I(X;Y,Z) = I(X;Z) + I(X;Y | Z)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfoOf_chain_rule_fst

declarations.doc: Random-variable form of `mutualInfo_chain_rule_snd`: `I(X;Y,Z) = I(X;Y) + I(X;Z | Y)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfoOf_chain_rule_snd

declarations.doc: Deterministic mutual-information chain rule: `I(X;Y) = I(f(X);Y) + I(X;Y | f(X))`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfoOf_deterministic_chain_rule_left

declarations.doc: Deterministic post-processing of the left variable cannot increase mutual information.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfoOf_comp_left_le

declarations.doc: Deterministic post-processing of the right variable cannot increase mutual information.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfoOf_comp_right_le

declarations.doc: Deterministic post-processing of both variables cannot increase mutual information.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfoOf_comp_le

declarations.doc: Mapping the first coordinate of a joint PMF cannot increase mutual information.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfo_map_fst_le

declarations.doc: Mapping the second coordinate of a joint PMF cannot increase mutual information.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfo_map_snd_le

declarations.doc: Mapping both coordinates of a joint PMF cannot increase mutual information.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfo_map_prod_le

declarations.doc: Explicitly two-sided alias for deterministic mutual-information processing.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfoOf_comp_both_le

declarations.doc: Left-coordinate alias for deterministic processing of a joint PMF.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfo_map_left_le

declarations.doc: Right-coordinate alias for deterministic processing of a joint PMF.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfo_map_right_le

declarations.doc: Both-coordinate alias for deterministic processing of a joint PMF.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.mutualInfo_map_both_le

declarations.doc: Conditional mutual information as a difference of conditional entropies: `I(A;B | C) = H(A | C) - H(A | B,C)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfo_eq_condEntropy_fstThirdMarginal_sub_condEntropy

declarations.doc: Symmetric conditional-entropy difference form: `I(A;B | C) = H(B | C) - H(B | A,C)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfo_eq_condEntropy_sndThirdMarginal_sub_condEntropy_swap12

declarations.doc: Conditioning reduces entropy: `H(A | B,C) <= H(A | C)`. The proof rewrites the difference as `I(A;B | C)` and uses conditional mutual information nonnegativity.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropy_le_condEntropy_fstThirdMarginal

declarations.doc: Random-variable conditioning-reduces-entropy theorem: `H(X | Y,Z) <= H(X | Z)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropyOf_pair_le_condEntropyOf

declarations.doc: Conditional mutual information is at most `H(A|C)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfo_le_condEntropy_fstThirdMarginal

declarations.doc: Conditional mutual information is at most `H(B|C)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfo_le_condEntropy_sndThirdMarginal

declarations.doc: `H(A|C)` is at most the conditional joint entropy `H(A,B|C)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropy_fstThirdMarginal_le_condEntropy_pairThirdLaw

declarations.doc: `H(B|C)` is at most the conditional joint entropy `H(A,B|C)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropy_sndThirdMarginal_le_condEntropy_pairThirdLaw

declarations.doc: Conditional joint entropy is subadditive: `H(A,B|C) <= H(A|C) + H(B|C)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropy_pairThirdLaw_le_condEntropy_fstThirdMarginal_add_condEntropy_sndThirdMarginal

declarations.doc: Random-variable conditional mutual information is at most `H(X|Z)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfoOf_le_condEntropyOf_left

declarations.doc: Random-variable conditional mutual information is at most `H(Y|Z)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condMutualInfoOf_le_condEntropyOf_right

declarations.doc: `H(X|Z)` is at most the conditional joint entropy `H(X,Y|Z)`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.condEntropyOf_le_condEntropyOf_pair

declarations.doc: `H(Y|Z)` is at most the conditional joint entropy `H(X,Y|Z)`.

declarations.doc: Change a real quantity from logarithm base `b` to logarithm base `c`. If `x` is measured in nats, then `x / Real.log b` is its value in base-`b` units. Thus the theorem is the usual change-of-base identity `x_c = log_c(b) * x_b`.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.div_log_change_base

declarations.doc: A `Real.negMulLog` summand in base-`b` units.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.negMulLog_div_log

declarations.doc: Finite PMF entropy converted from nats to base-`b` units is the usual `-sum_x p(x) log_b p(x)` formula.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropy_div_log

declarations.doc: Finite-valued random-variable entropy converted from nats to base-`b` units.
declarations.name: LeanInfoTheory.Shannon.entropyOf_div_log
